title: PR-2 autonomous town life architecture and reviewed task DAG
summary: Rebaseline post-PR-1 product truth, define autonomous resident behavior and presentation contracts, reconcile legacy control-plane wording, and create a finite reviewed PR-2 implementation/integration/product-acceptance DAG without product code.
acceptance: Project truth and a structured scorecard record PR-1 as independently verified/integrated/done and PR-2 as planning-only; integrated prototype is the highest earned label, while autonomous prototype, internal playable, save and release readiness remain unearned

Architecture defines one authoritative eight-resident 64x64 minimum scenario across a 36000-tick full game day using needs, staggered schedules, capabilities, indexed Top-K work selection, versioned pathing, atomic reservations and explicit reusable life/work drivers

Architecture defines serializable idle/claiming/moving/working/blocked/completed/failed/interrupted states, structured reason/suggestion taxonomy, separate pause plus honest 1x/2x/4x=30/60/120 TPS Worker semantics, and read-only projection/interpolation/HUD ownership

ADR-0018 documents why schema3/projection-v1 and actual 0/1/2/3 multiplier semantics are insufficient; it approves only future schema4/projection-v2 implementation by WM-0172 with exact fields, compatibility/fail-closed rules, default-module v1 isolation and unchanged save boundary

Evidence plan combines deterministic 36000/100000-tick simulation, real module Worker/default Web interaction and uninterrupted behavior capture, performance/conservation metrics, and independent gameplay-designer plus reviewer observation; debug payload alone cannot pass

Inventory classifies PR-1 and historical M1-M8 stores, drivers, fixtures, projections and save surfaces into reuse/adapt/retire/test-only, with missing product integration and performance boundaries explicit

A finite executable WM-0170..WM-0175 PR-2 DAG has strict serial dependencies, exact file ownership, complete task packets, integration/automated evidence and independent product acceptance; maximum concurrent write-heavy width is one
WM-0154 blocker wording is reconciled to current PR-1 facts while WM-0154 remains blocked and WM-0155/WM-0156 remain frozen until their roadmap gates
No product implementation, protocol/schema/save mutation, implementation claim, release action or PR-3+ implementation is executed

history.details: Recorded WM-0166 as the completed PR-1 predecessor. WM-0169 remains ready because that dependency was already independently verified, integrated and done before task creation.

Recorded ADR-0018 and the autonomous-town architecture; proposed strict serial WM-0170..WM-0175 core/integration/protocol/presentation/evidence/product-acceptance DAG with maximum write width one. All implementation tasks remain proposed and unclaimed.

proposedProtocolOwner.scope: ADR-0018 envelope schema4, GameSession projection v2, explicit speedMultiplier and default schema4/v2 negotiation

saveLoadStance: PR-2 planning may preserve and classify the authoritative save boundary, but it does not implement GameSession save/load, mutate a save format or make a public compatibility promise.
forbiddenPaths: claiming or promoting PR-2 implementation tasks before WM-0169 is independently verified, integrated and done
unblocking or claiming WM-0154, WM-0155 or WM-0156
creating or claiming PR-3+ implementation tasks
protocol/schema/save mutation without a separate explicit reviewed decision and exclusive task ownership
public release, Early Access, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public save compatibility actions
singleWriterRule: WM-0169 is the sole PR-2 planning writer. Every proposed implementation, integration and product-acceptance task must remain proposed and unclaimed until this planning task is independently verified, integrated and done.
